subject: WuXi Biologics <-> WuXi AppTec inter-group transaction nature
sought: Whether the disclosed related-party transactions between WuXi Biologics and WuXi AppTec entities (WASZ, WXAT Shanghai, STA R&D, WuXi ATU, STA Changzhou) constitute API/drug-substance supply, CDMO manufacturing, or pure lab-testing services -- i.e. whether either company depends on the other for a chokepoint capability.
searched: https://doc.irasia.com/listco/hk/wuxibiologics/annual/2025/ar2025.pdf
found_instead: Note 43 "RELATED PARTY TRANSACTIONS AND BALANCES" lines items only generically: "Sales of materials to related parties" (WuXi ATU RMB367k, WATJ RMB22k, STA Changzhou RMB(2024)85k), "Testing service received" (WASZ RMB53,613k, STA R&D RMB53,105k, WXAT Shanghai RMB41,421k, WuXi ATU RMB3,839k), "Provision of research and development service to related parties" (STA RMB8,118k, STA Wuxi RMB1,992k). No line item says what the "materials" are (API? excipient? equipment?) or what the R&D/testing service covers. WuXi Biologics and WuXi AppTec share a common founder/chairman (Dr. Ge Li) and are disclosed as related parties, but this filing never characterizes the transactions as API supply or CDMO manufacturing -- did not force an edge on a guess.
blocks: hypothesis-1 and hypothesis-2 both -- this is the one place in the whole document set where two roster companies are confirmed to transact with each other, and the filing still declines to say what for.
would_close_it: WuXi AppTec's own related-party note (mirror disclosure) or either company's segment-level intercompany-sales breakdown, if one exists.

subject: BIOSECURE Act, current status
sought: Whether the BIOSECURE Act is still live legislation, which is this map's stated test of how substitutable WuXi Biologics is
searched: congress.gov H.R.8333 (118th) full bill text, read in a browser 2026-07-22; congress.gov S.3558 (118th) full text; congress.gov legislation search for the 119th Congress
found_instead: THE TEXT WAS REACHED and it settles the naming question: H.R.8333, short title 'BIOSECURE Act', defines 'biotechnology company of concern' as 'BGI, MGI, Complete Genomics, WuXi AppTec, and WuXi Biologics' -- so FOUNDATION.md's claim that WuXi Biologics is explicitly named is CORRECT. What could NOT be established is STATUS. H.R.8333's last action was 2024-09-10, referred to Senate committee, and the 118th Congress ended in January 2025, so that bill died. Whether it was reintroduced in the 119th is unknown: congress.gov's search returns a Cloudflare bot-detection interstitial, which was deliberately NOT bypassed. Note also that S.3558, often called BIOSECURE, is a DIFFERENT bill that names WuXi AppTec only.
blocks: the map calls BIOSECURE 'proposed', which a 2026 reader reads as pending; the bill that names WuXi Biologics is dead in the Congress that produced it
would_close_it: a congress.gov 119th-Congress bill page opened by a human, or the Congress.gov API

subject: Samsung Biologics and WuXi AppTec as CDMO manufacturers for any of the ten originators on this map
sought: A named manufactures-for relationship between Samsung Biologics or WuXi AppTec and any roster originator (Pfizer, J&J, Roche, Novartis, Merck, AstraZeneca, Sanofi, Eli Lilly, AbbVie, Novo Nordisk) or Teva.
searched: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/78003/000007800326000026/pfe-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/200406/000020040626000016/jnj-20251228.htm; https://assets.roche.com/f/176343/x/fca190f63e/fb25e.pdf; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1114448/000111444826000004/nvs-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/310158/000031015826000063/mrk-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/901832/000110465926019130/azn-20251231x20f.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1121404/000162828026008403/sny-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/59478/000005947826000013/lly-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1551152/000155115226000008/abbv-20251231.htm; https://www.novonordisk.com/content/dam/nncorp/global/en/investors/irmaterial/annual_report/2026/novo-nordisk-annual-report-2025.pdf; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/818686/000119312526034532/d93612d10k.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1596783/000159678324000087/ctlt-20240630.htm
found_instead: Zero occurrences of the string "Samsung Biologics" or "WuXi AppTec" across all twelve documents. The only CDMO relationships any of these filings actually name are Lonza (Sanofi's BioAtrium JV, Roche's Vacaville divestment/manufacturing agreement), WuXi Biologics (Merck's Dundalk facility acquisition from WuXi Vaccines, its subsidiary), and Catalent (named as a CDMO customer relationship with Pfizer and Novo Nordisk in Catalent's own 10-K).
blocks: manufactures-for edges for Samsung Biologics and WuXi AppTec anywhere on this map.
would_close_it: Samsung Biologics' or WuXi AppTec's own annual report / 20-F naming a specific originator customer, the same recipe that worked for Catalent's own 10-K; or an originator's manufacturing-network / supply-chain investor disclosure naming either CDMO.

subject: Zhejiang Huahai Pharmaceutical, Scientific Protein Laboratories, Sun Pharmaceutical Industries, Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, Aurobindo Pharma, and Cipla as active-pharmaceutical-ingredient (API) suppliers to any of the ten originators
sought: A supplies-api-to relationship: an originator's own filing naming one of these six as an active ingredient or intermediate supplier.
searched: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/78003/000007800326000026/pfe-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/200406/000020040626000016/jnj-20251228.htm; https://assets.roche.com/f/176343/x/fca190f63e/fb25e.pdf; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1114448/000111444826000004/nvs-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/310158/000031015826000063/mrk-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/901832/000110465926019130/azn-20251231x20f.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1121404/000162828026008403/sny-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/59478/000005947826000013/lly-20251231.htm; https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1551152/000155115226000008/abbv-20251231.htm; https://www.novonordisk.com/content/dam/nncorp/global/en/investors/irmaterial/annual_report/2026/novo-nordisk-annual-report-2025.pdf
found_instead: Zhejiang Huahai Pharmaceutical and Scientific Protein Laboratories do not appear at all in any of the ten originator filings. Sun Pharmaceutical Industries, Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, Aurobindo Pharma, and Cipla appear only as ANDA-filing generic COMPETITORS in patent-litigation sections (e.g. Pfizer v. Aurobindo on Nurtec, J&J v. Dr. Reddy's/Cipla on Xarelto, AbbVie v. Aurobindo/Dr. Reddy's on Ubrelvy/Qulipta, AstraZeneca v. Cipla/Sun Pharma on Calquence/Forxiga) -- never as suppliers. This mirrors the sister-map finding that no hyperscaler names its main chip supplier in its own 10-K: API sourcing is a fact originators disclose even less than manufacturing.
blocks: Every supplies-api-to edge on this map; the API-supply layer of the pharma chokepoint hypothesis is currently untestable from the originator side.
would_close_it: An originator's supply-chain-resilience or API-sourcing disclosure (rare but occasionally appears in ESG/sustainability reports rather than the 10-K/20-F); an FDA Drug Master File cross-reference; or the API maker's own annual report naming a customer by name.

subject: Scientific Protein Laboratories
sought: primary-source evidence that this map's flagged chokepoint node is the entity under FDA import detention for crude heparin
searched: FDA Import Alert 66-40 'Detention Without Physical Examination of Drugs From Firms Which Have Not Met Drug GMPs', full text as published 2026-07-21; the eight API/CDMO documents read in this burst
found_instead: The alert DOES carry a live red-list entry for 'Changzhou SPL Company, Ltd.', West Rd., Hutang Township, Wujin City, Changzhou, Jiangsu, CHINA, covering Crude Heparin and all heparin products, published 2009-09-10 with a 2008-03-10 date -- the contaminated-heparin episode this map's FOUNDATION.md cites. But the alert never writes 'Scientific Protein Laboratories': measured zero occurrences, alongside zero for Huahai and zero for Hepalink. So the document that would evidence the chokepoint names a plant, and no document opened in this burst binds that plant to the roster node.
blocks: the crude-heparin leg of chokepoint hypothesis 1 -- the strongest available evidence cannot be attached to the node without an identity binding
would_close_it: an SEC/Hepalink filing, or an FDA document, that states the corporate relationship between Changzhou SPL Company Ltd and Scientific Protein Laboratories

subject: Catalent
sought: current confirmation that Catalent still manufactures for Pfizer and Novo Nordisk, the two manufactures-for edges this map draws from it
searched: Catalent's FY2024 Form 10-K for the year ended 2024-06-30 (ctlt-20240630.htm), which is the LAST annual report Catalent filed and the source of both edges; this map's own companies.csv row for Catalent
found_instead: Both edges rest on one sentence, 'Selected key customers include Bayer, Bristol-Myers Squibb, GlaxoSmithKline, Haleon, Novo Nordisk, Moderna, Pfizer, and Sarepta Therapeutics.' That sentence is true as filed, but it predates a material change to the counterparty: Novo Holdings took Catalent private in December 2024, and three Catalent fill-finish sites were sold on to Novo Nordisk. As a private company Catalent files no further 10-K, so no newer disclosure can refresh or retract this. The edges are kept rather than deleted -- deleting would destroy a sourced fact -- but they are dated 2024-06-30 and a reader should not take them as current.
blocks: two of this map's six manufactures-for edges, and part of the CDMO chokepoint hypothesis, rest on evidence that can never be refreshed from the same source
would_close_it: a Novo Holdings or Novo Nordisk disclosure describing Catalent's post-acquisition customer base, or a Pfizer filing naming Catalent as a manufacturer

subject: CVS Health (Caremark); Cigna (Express Scripts); UnitedHealth Group (OptumRx)
sought: a manufacturer-specific rebate or formulary negotiation between a named PBM and a named manufacturer, to carry the three negotiates-rebates-with edges this map draws
searched: Sanofi's FY2025 Form 20-F risk factors, which is the cited source for all three edges; the FY2025 10-Ks of CVS Health, Cigna and UnitedHealth Group, which were read for rebate counterparties and disclose only accounting mechanics
found_instead: Sanofi's filing names all three PBMs in one passage about formulary-exclusion leverage -- 'the three largest pharmacy benefit managers (PBMs) - Caremark (CVS Health), Express Scripts (Cigna), and OptumRx (United Health Group) - have again each excluded 600 or more drugs from their formularies' -- but frames it as an industry condition affecting manufacturers generally, NOT as a Sanofi-specific negotiated rebate. The three edges are therefore weaker than their existence implies and are marked medium confidence. They are the first rows that should be re-examined or dropped.
blocks: the PBM chokepoint's only relationship edges rest on a generic industry statement rather than a bilateral disclosure
would_close_it: any manufacturer's filing disclosing a named PBM rebate arrangement or a formulary exclusion affecting a specific product, or a PBM filing naming a manufacturer counterparty
